testFuzz_slice_lengthOverflows_reverts(b.notice: Tests that, when given a length `n` that is greater than `type(uint256).max - 31`,         the `slice` function reverts.

testFuzz_slice_memorySafety_succeeds(byt.notice: Tests that the `slice` function correctly updates the free memory pointer depending         on the length of the slice.         The calls to `bound` are to reduce the number of times that `assume` is triggered.

testFuzz_slice_outOfBounds_reverts(bytes.notice: Tests that, when given an input bytes array of length `n`, the `slice` function         will always revert if `_start + _length > n`.

testFuzz_slice_rangeOverflows_reverts(by.notice: Tests that, when given a start index `n` that is greater than         `type(uint256).max - n`, the `slice` function reverts.         The calls to `bound` are to reduce the number of times that `assume` is triggered.

test_slice_acrossMultipleWords_works().notice: Tests that the `slice` function works as expected when slicing between multiple         words in memory. In this case, we test that a 34 byte slice between 3 separate         words returns the correct result.

test_slice_acrossWords_works().notice: Tests that the `slice` function works as expected when slicing between multiple         words in memory. In this case, we test that a 2 byte slice between the 32nd byte of         the first word and the 1st byte of the second word is correct.

test_slice_fromNonZeroIdx_works().notice: Tests that the `slice` function works as expected when starting from indices [1, 9]         with lengths [1, 9], in reverse order.

test_slice_fromZeroIdx_works().notice: Tests that the `slice` function works as expected when starting from index 0.

testFuzz_slice_lengthOverflows_reverts(b.notice: Tests that, when given a length `n` that is greater than `type(uint256).max - 31`,         the `slice` function reverts.

testFuzz_slice_memorySafety_succeeds(byt.notice: Tests that the `slice` function correctly updates the free memory pointer depending         on the length of the slice.         The calls to `bound` are to reduce the number of times that `assume` is triggered.

testFuzz_slice_outOfBounds_reverts(bytes.notice: Tests that, when given an input bytes array of length `n`, the `slice` function         will always revert if `_start + _length > n`.

testFuzz_slice_rangeOverflows_reverts(by.notice: Tests that, when given a start index `n` that is greater than         `type(uint256).max - n`, the `slice` function reverts.         The calls to `bound` are to reduce the number of times that `assume` is triggered.

test_slice_acrossMultipleWords_works().notice: Tests that the `slice` function works as expected when slicing between multiple         words in memory. In this case, we test that a 34 byte slice between 3 separate         words returns the correct result.

test_slice_acrossWords_works().notice: Tests that the `slice` function works as expected when slicing between multiple         words in memory. In this case, we test that a 2 byte slice between the 32nd byte of         the first word and the 1st byte of the second word is correct.

test_slice_fromNonZeroIdx_works().notice: Tests that the `slice` function works as expected when starting from indices [1, 9]         with lengths [1, 9], in reverse order.

test_slice_fromZeroIdx_works().notice: Tests that the `slice` function works as expected when starting from index 0.

userdoc.notice: Tests the `slice` function of the `Bytes` library.

documentation.text:
@title Bytes_TestInit
 @notice Reusable test initialization for `Bytes` tests.
@notice Tests that the `slice` function works as expected when starting from index 0.

@notice Tests that the `slice` function works as expected when starting from indices [1, 9]
         with lengths [1, 9], in reverse order.

         will always revert if `_start + _length > n`.

documentation.text:
@title Bytes_Slice_Test
 @notice Tests the `slice` function of the `Bytes` library.

@notice Tests that, given an input of 5 bytes, the `toNibbles` function returns an array of
         10 nibbles corresponding to the input data.

@notice Tests that, given an input of 0 bytes, the `toNibbles` function returns a zero
         length array.

@notice Tests that the `toNibbles` function correctly updates the free memory pointer
         depending on the length of the resulting array.

@title Bytes_ToNibbles_Test
 @notice Tests the `toNibbles` function of the `Bytes` library.

@notice Tests that the `equal` function in the `Bytes` library returns `false` if given two
         non-equal byte arrays.

@notice Test whether or not the `equal` function in the `Bytes` library is equivalent to
         manually checking equality of the two dynamic `bytes` arrays in memory.

@title Bytes_Equal_Test
 @notice Tests the `equal` function of the `Bytes` library.
